channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Turner Isoun's legacy: How Nigeria's digital revolution began
item.description: Turner Timinipre Isoun, a former Nigerian minister of Science and Technology, passed away at 87, leaving behind a legacy that transformed the country's digital landscape. His policies during the Obasa...

item.title: Tanzania enhances efforts to tackle human trafficking
item.description: Tanzania has intensified its measures to address human trafficking through comprehensive strategies aimed at prevention, protection, and prosecution. The government has collaborated with local and int...

item.title: Bahati Responds to Organ Harvesting Allegations with Leaked Police Report
item.description: Celebrated Kenyan musician Bahati has addressed claims that he harvests organs from young women, sharing a leaked police report involving the same man who accused him. The controversy began when Kevin...

item.title: Media prank: Bruce Bouchet's $7 domain purchase sparked false collaboration rumors between Kyle Sandilands and Karl Stefanovic
item.description: Bruce Bouchet, long-time manager of shock jock Kyle Sandilands, has admitted he orchestrated the infamous rumor that the controversial media personalities were collaborating on a new project. The pran...

item.title: Asiatic Lions Rely on Wild Prey, Study Shows
item.description: A study published in Conservation journal reveals that Asiatic lions in Gujarat's coastal regions primarily rely on wild prey rather than livestock. Researchers analyzed 160 lion scat samples from Jun...

item.title: France dominate Japan 42-15 in Tokyo rugby clash
item.description: In a high-tempo rugby encounter at the National Stadium in Tokyo, France secured a decisive 42-15 victory over Japan, marking their first leg of the inaugural Nations Championship. The Six Nations hol...

item.title: Protester faces multiple police charges over protest actions, with authorities citing misconduct investigations
item.description: Ms Barnes, an activist involved in environmental protests, has faced numerous legal cases against her by Norfolk Police between 2020 and 2024. While she received a conditional discharge for criminal d...

item.title: Republican Senator Considers Run After Trump Endorsement Snub
item.description: Republican Senator Russell Fry of South Carolina expressed uncertainty about his political future after being overlooked by President Donald Trump during his endorsement of Darline Graham Nordone for ...

item.title: DPM Dar and Kuwait FM Discuss Regional Stability and Islamabad MoU Compliance
item.description: Deputy Prime Minister Ishaq Dar engaged in a critical phone call with Kuwaiti Foreign Minister Sheikh Jarrah Jaber Al-Ahmad Al-Sabah to address escalating tensions in the Middle East. The discussion c...

item.title: Uganda and Tanzania leaders discuss media closure resolution
item.description: Tanzanian businessman Rostam Azizi, set to become majority shareholder of Nation Media Group (NMG), met Ugandan President Yoweri Museveni to address the shutdown of NMG Uganda's operations. The meetin...

item.title: Multinational Cybersecurity Threats and Data Privacy Concerns Highlighted in Weekly Security Report
item.description: The article provides a comprehensive overview of recent cybersecurity developments, emphasizing privacy risks and data breaches. Key topics include concerns about period-tracking apps potentially coll...

item.title: Edmonton police investigate sudden infant death
item.description: Edmonton police are investigating the sudden death of an infant who was found unresponsive at a residence on the northeast side of the city. The incident occurred on Friday, July 17, 2026, when emerge...

item.title: Journalist Rose Mbugua Dies in Motorcycle Accident
item.description: A video capturing the aftermath of the fatal crash that killed journalist Rose Mbugua has gone viral, sparking nationwide mourning in Kenya. The incident, which occurred on Uhuru Highway near the Univ...

item.title: Pakistan extends airspace restrictions on Indian flights until August 23
item.description: Pakistan has extended its airspace ban on Indian civilian and military aircraft for another month, until August 23, 2026. The restriction, imposed by the Pakistan Airports Authority (PAA), applies to ...

item.title: Andhra Pradesh CM Promotes Data Centre Development
item.description: The article discusses Andhra Pradesh Chief Minister N. Chandrababu Naidu's initiatives to establish Visakhapatnam as a data centre hub. Key projects include Google's $15 billion AI Hub near Visakhapat...

item.title: Mpumalanga Official Dismissed for Corruption in COVID-19 Disinfection Procurement
item.description: A former state accountant in Mpumalanga's Department of Public Works, Roads and Transport, Jacob Bandile Ngcobo, has been dismissed following a Special Investigating Unit (SIU) probe into corruption l...

item.title: Blood of Dawnwalker to Release Full Game Data on Physical Disc
item.description: Bandai Namco and Rebel Wolves have confirmed that Blood of Dawnwalker, a medieval vampire role-playing game, will be released on physical disc rather than digital-only. The decision comes amid industr...

item.title: Survivor recounts family massacre in Plateau State attack
item.description: A survivor of a brutal attack in Kum village, Rim District, Plateau State, has shared harrowing details of how nine family members were killed during the assault. James Yohanna, one of three survivors...

item.title: SportyTV's World Cup Coverage Surpasses 1 Billion Views
item.description: SportyTV has recorded over 1 billion views during the 2026 FIFA World Cup, showcasing the growing demand for digital-first sports content. The broadcaster attributed this success to its interactive fa...

item.title: Ex-Speaker Ready to Face Ombudsman Probe, Denies Links to Flood Control Anomalies
item.description: The Romualdez camp has stated that the former Speaker is prepared to undergo an Ombudsman investigation, while firmly denying any connections to irregularities in flood control projects. The announcem...
